author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

Added bounded-retry policy with exponential backoff to the
storage layer's CAS loop. Three call sites used the prior
single-retry `jittered_retry_sleep` helper:
`Storage::add_comment`, `Storage::mutate` (covers
set_title/set_status/claim/block/label/dep/...), and the
slug-claim path in `create_issue` (kept fail-fast — slug
re-races would loop indefinitely).

New `RetryPolicy::from_env()` reads `JJF_MAX_RETRIES` (default
5, clamp 0..=20) and `JJF_RETRY_BASE_MS` (default 10, clamp
0..=10000). The geometric schedule [1, 2, 6, 15, 35, 85] ×
base_ms gives 10/20/60/150/350 ms with ~25% jitter derived
from wall-clock nanos XOR pid (no `rand` dep). When base_ms=0,
no sleeps fire (instant retry). The exhausted-hint mentions
the configured count: `"retried 5 times and still raced"`.

Files: `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs` (helper + two retry
sites rewritten), `crates/jjf-storage/tests/integration.rs`
(+4 tests), `crates/jjf/tests/comment.rs` (+1 CLI test).

## Recommendation

Closed. The 10-way QA repro now lands 7/10 with the default
budget (was ~2). Five-way racers land 5/5 reliably. Losers
under 10-way burst still get a clean typed
`concurrent_write` and can retry their command.

## Confidence

High — 474 workspace tests green; QA repro verified.

## Open follow-ups

None — the original ticket noted candidates 2/3 (retry knob,
documented retry contract) as "probably not needed if (1) is
enough." JJF_MAX_RETRIES already surfaces the knob for the
rare overflow case.
